html:
[201]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 之先勅神将呼土地诘问 - A series of actions involving commands and actions taken by a divine figure: '之前' (before), '勅' (command), '神将' (divine generals), '呼' (call), '土地' (land deity), '诘问' (interrogate).
  • 有降神小童见其至咄咄自语 - A spirit child's reaction to the arrival of a divine figure: '降神小童' (a child who has descended as a spirit), '见其至' (saw him arrive), '咄咄自语' (muttered to himself).
  • 官府严整如何得有邪祟 - A rhetorical question expressing disbelief: '官府严整' (the official government is strict and orderly), '如何得有邪祟' (how could there be evil spirits?).
  • 恐是他家婢妾之属所为 - A possible explanation for the presence of evil spirits: '恐是' (probably), '他家婢妾之属' (from his household, servants or concubines), '所为' (what they did).
  • 那可责我实无可捕 - An expression of frustration and lack of options: '那可责我' (how can I be blamed), '实无可捕' (there are no ghosts to capture).
  • 姚谢去自于其家飞符噀水 - Actions taken by Yao: '姚谢去' (Yao left), '自于其家' (from his own home), '飞符噀水' (flew talismans and sprayed water).
  • 凡十余日摄出一女子盖迅母也 - The outcome of Yao's actions: '凡十余日' (for more than ten days), '摄出一女子' (captured a woman), '盖迅母也' (it turned out to be his mother).
  • 不肯言所以来 - The woman's reluctance to explain her actions: '不肯言' (would not speak), '所以来' (why she came).
  • 姚牒城隍寄収 - Legal action taken by Yao: '姚牒' (Yao issued a document), '城隍' (City God), '寄収' (to hold in custody).
  • 他日再呼问始云 - Subsequent questioning: '他日' (another day), '再呼问' (called again to ask), '始云' (finally said).
  • 因小官人来范家故随入县舍 - Explanation of the woman's actions: '因小官人来范家' (because the young official came to the Fan family), '故随入县舍' (so she followed him into the county office).
  • 以酒馔香楮遣之 - Ritual performed to send the woman away: '以酒馔香楮' (with wine, food, incense, and paper money), '遣之' (sent her away).
  • 焚其柩防即愈 - Immediate effect of the ritual: '焚其柩' (burned her coffin), '防即愈' (the illness immediately improved).
  • 澧州判官 - Identifies the official in question: '澧州' (Li Prefecture), '判官' (judge or official).
  • 故郭邵州沼之子林字伯賨 - Identifies the official's background: '故郭邵州沼' (the late Governor De of Shaozhou), '之子林' (his son Lin), '字伯賨' (style name Bocong).
  • 绍熈二年自鄱阳赴澧州判官莅事数月 - Provides the timeline and location of the official's service: '绍熈二年' (in the second year of the Shaoxi era), '自鄱阳' (from Poyang), '赴澧州判官' (to become a judge in Li Prefecture), '莅事数月' (served for several months).
  • 直宿兵卒见本衙土地庙判官踞坐防上 - Describes an unusual sight: '直宿兵卒' (night watch soldiers), '见本衙土地庙' (saw the temple of the land deity in the courthouse), '判官踞坐防上' (the judge sitting arrogantly on the altar).
  • 或以告林林意诸卒相戏不以为怪 - Describes Lin's initial reaction: '或以告林' (someone told Lin), '林意诸卒相戏' (Lin thought the soldiers were joking), '不以为怪' (did not find it strange).
  • 次夜复然隂察之了无迹 - Describes the repeated observation: '次夜复然' (the next night again), '隂察之' (observed secretly), '了无迹' (found no trace).
  • 但杖阍者而亲往闭庙门 - Describes the actions taken by Lin: '但杖阍者' (only beat the gatekeeper), '而亲往闭庙门' (and personally went to close the temple door).
  • 且选一谨信者宿其侧 - Describes the security measures taken: '且选一谨信者' (also selected a trustworthy person), '宿其侧' (to stay beside the temple).
  • 此人惧谴终夕不敢寝 - Describes the person's fear: '此人惧谴' (this person feared punishment), '终夕不敢寝' (did not dare sleep all night).
  • 将五更见一人身躯颇伟髭髯如防从外来践履其衾席而过 - Describes the appearance and actions of the mysterious figure: '将五更' (just before dawn), '见一人身躯颇伟' (saw a person with a large body), '髭髯如防' (with a beard like a horse's mane), '从外来践履其衾席而过' (who came from outside and stepped on his bedding).
  • 至旦则俨然据主位偃蹇 - Describes the figure's position: '至旦' (by morning), '则俨然据主位' (he was already sitting in the main position), '偃蹇' (arrogantly).
  • 林亟验封钥门闭如故 - Describes Lin's verification: '林亟验封钥' (Lin quickly checked the seals and locks), '门闭如故' (the door was still closed as before).
  • 始大骇讶曰得非奉事不肃招致此异 - Describes Lin's realization: '始大骇讶' (finally greatly frightened and surprised), '曰得非奉事不肃' (said it must be because the worship was not respectful), '招致此异' (caused this strange phenomenon).
  • 乃具牲酒拜祷谢过 - Describes the ritual performed by Lin: '乃具牲酒' (then prepared meat and wine), '拜祷谢过' (worshiped and apologized for the mistake).
  • 驭者拾鞭复进已颓仆马背 - Describes the aftermath: '驭者拾鞭复进' (the driver picked up the whip and continued), '已颓仆马背' (Lin had already fallen off the horse's back).
  • 扶掖以下仅能拜诏甫归家即死 - Describes Lin's final moments: '扶掖以下' (was helped down), '仅能拜诏' (could only bow to the edict), '甫归家即死' (as soon as he returned home, he died).
  • 防吏方云数日前梦本官与庙判官为代 - Describes a dream experienced by the guards: '防吏方云' (the guards said), '数日前梦本官与庙判官为代' (a few days ago, they dreamed that the official and the temple judge had switched places).
  • 林本雅素吉人而邂逅至此虽殁而为神亦眇末矣 - Describes the final judgment on Lin's fate: '林本雅素吉人' (Lin was originally a virtuous and auspicious person), '而邂逅至此' (but met with this fate), '虽殁而为神亦眇末矣' (even after death, he became a minor deity).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍熈二年 - The second year of the Shaoxi era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 澧州 - Li Prefecture, a historical administrative division in China.
  • 郭邵州沼 - The late Governor De of Shaozhou, a historical figure.
  • 泰山府 - Tian Shan Temple, a place of worship in Chinese culture.
  • 城隍 - City God, a deity in Chinese folklore.

[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 臨江二異 - Title that translates to 'Two Strange Events in Linjiang,' where '異' means 'strange' or 'unusual.'
  • 出則以前足抱頭而睢盱 - Describes the cat's behavior: 'when it comes out, it holds its head with its front paws and looks around with a glaring expression.' This is a vivid description of the cat's unusual posture and demeanor.
  • 省倉內白犬 - Refers to a white dog inside the provincial granary, which is considered a bad omen.
  • 見之者必有災咎 - Means 'those who see it will surely suffer misfortune or disaster.'
  • 通判庭 - Refers to the magistrate's office, where the cat carried its young.
  • 支馬谷 - Refers to the grain stored for horses, indicating a specific type of grain storage.
  • 稽叩連拜 - Describes the soldiers' behavior: 'kneeling, bowing repeatedly,' showing their reverence or fear.
  • 望外而立 - Means 'standing outside the pile,' indicating the dog's position relative to the grain.
  • 朱司法妾 - Refers to the concubine of Zhu Cong, a judicial official.
  • 自刎 - Means 'committed suicide' or 'cut her own throat.'
  • 從今日以後不可往岳殿 - Means 'from today onward, you must not go to the Yue Temple,' a command from the Taoist to the ghost.
  • 忿恚溢面 - Describes the ghost's expression: 'anger and resentment were evident on her face.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 臨江軍 - Refers to Linjiang, a military district in Jiangxi Province during the Song Dynasty.
  • 景韋兄 - Refers to a person named Jing Wei, who was likely a high-ranking official or a relative of the author.
  • 曹使 - Refers to a censor or inspector, a position responsible for overseeing local officials.
  • 清江尉 - Refers to a magistrate in Qingjiang, a county in Jiangxi.
  • 閣皀山道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest from Ge Sha Mountain, known for performing exorcisms and rituals.
  • 城隍廟 - Refers to the Temple of the City God, a common place of worship in Chinese cities where spirits and deities were believed to reside.
  • 岳殿 - Refers to the Yue Temple, dedicated to the God of Mount Hua, one of the Four Sacred Mountains of Taoism.

Proper Nouns

  • 臨江軍 - A military district in Jiangxi.
  • 景韋兄 - A person named Jing Wei, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 曹使 - A censor or inspector.
  • 清江尉 - A magistrate in Qingjiang.
  • 閣皀山道士 - A Taoist priest from Ge Sha Mountain.
  • 城隍廟 - The Temple of the City God.
  • 岳殿 - The Yue Temple.
  • 朱琮 - The name of the judicial official mentioned in the second part of the text.

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 做官人侍婢时安得致此 - A set phrase meaning 'How could a servant of an official acquire this?' or 'How could a servant of an official come to possess this?' The context suggests that the servant acquired something unusual or significant.
  • 还舍以告妻 - Literally means 'returning home to tell his wife.' This indicates a personal action taken by the servant.
  • 未几遇疾卒 - Means 'not long after, he fell ill and died.' This phrase describes a rapid sequence of events.
  • 王氏旋踵并亡 - Means 'Wang's wife died shortly after.' The term '旋踵' (turning the heel) is a metaphor for a very short time.
  • 凡生三男子大者才六岁 - Means 'He had three sons, the eldest being only six years old.' This provides information about the family's situation.
  • 无人主丧族姻有从官邻者闻之亟传檄来为料理后事 - A complex phrase that can be broken down as follows: 'No one to manage the funeral.' 'Relatives and neighbors who were officials.' 'Upon hearing this, they quickly sent a message to arrange the funeral affairs.'
  • 亦尝招道流考召见朱着袍执简立二女囚荷枷被讯 - A complex phrase that can be broken down as follows: 'Also summoned a Daoist to examine.' 'Seeing Zhu dressed in a robe holding a tablet, standing with two female prisoners wearing shackles and being questioned.'
  • 迨棺柩出门邻家室女见两棺后一妇人蓬头敝衣抚掌大笑相续去女惊异为父母说即时病喑竟不复能语 - A very detailed and complex phrase: 'When the coffin was taken out.' 'A neighbor's daughter saw two coffins followed by a woman with disheveled hair and ragged clothes clapping and laughing, then leaving.' 'The girl was shocked and told her parents, who immediately fell ill and became mute, never speaking again.'
  • 朱里人黄祥主簿说予谓一妾之死而主君主母偿其命岳庙之严而不致摄一使之出入自若小女子话其异即病喑是皆可疑也 - A complex phrase: 'Zhu's neighbor, Huang Xiang, a local official, said to me that he thought...' 'The death of one concubine and the master and mistress paying for her life.' 'The strictness of the temple and not capturing a ghost, allowing it to move freely.' 'A young girl telling of the strange events immediately fell ill and became mute, all of which are suspicious.'
  • 弋阳女子 - A proper noun referring to a specific incident involving a girl from Yiyang.
  • 弋阳陈秀才与其友以元夕观灯于市 - Means 'A scholar from Yiyang named Chen and his friend went to watch lanterns on the Lantern Festival night.'
  • 有人家女子年十三四岁坐仆肩右坠髻花一枝正落陈巾上陈甚喜携归示妻 - A detailed description: 'A girl from a household, about 13 or 14 years old.' 'Sitting on the shoulder of a servant, a hairpin fell from her hair and landed on Chen's hat.' 'Chen was very happy and took her home to show his wife.'
  • 妻疑其游倡馆所得夺而掷于地 - Means 'His wife suspected that he had obtained her from a brothel and took the hairpin away, throwing it on the ground.'
  • 陈邀其友证所以妻愀然不乐曰果若汝言当是异事是兆祥我死不久矣 - A complex phrase: 'Chen invited his friend to verify the reason.' 'His wife looked sad and unhappy, saying...' 'If what you say is true, it must be an unusual event, a sign of good fortune, and I will not live long.'
  • 后数月竟卒陈鳏居十年始别娶复因灯夕游观戏举前事盖坠花者也 - A detailed sequence: 'After several months, she died.' 'Chen remained single for ten years before remarrying.' 'He again went to watch lanterns on the Lantern Festival night and mentioned the previous incident.' 'It was the girl who dropped the hairpin.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 做官人 - Refers to a servant of an official, indicating a social hierarchy.
  • 侍婢 - Refers to a female servant, often in a household.
  • 元夕 - The Lantern Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated on the 15th day of the first lunar month.
  • 倡馆 - A brothel or place where courtesans were kept.
  • 岳庙 - A temple dedicated to the goddess of marriage, often associated with fertility and protection.
  • 道流 - Refers to Daoist priests or practitioners.
  • 蓬头敝衣 - Describes a disheveled and ragged appearance, often associated with supernatural beings or ghosts.
  • 抚掌大笑 - Describes a gesture of clapping and laughing, often used to indicate a supernatural or ghostly presence.
  • 病喑 - Refers to a condition of being mute due to illness.

Proper Nouns

  • 弋阳女子 - A proper noun referring to a specific incident involving a girl from Yiyang.
  • 弋阳陈秀才 - A scholar from Yiyang named Chen.
  • 黄祥主簿 - Zhu's neighbor, Huang Xiang, a local official.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 劝善大师 - Title for a deity or spiritual figure, meaning 'Master Who Encourages Good Deeds'.
  • 遭火荡尽 - Completely destroyed by fire.
  • 草创修理 - Roughly repaired or restored.
  • 有整治之意 - Had the intention to renovate.
  • 蔽塞通逵 - Blocking the thoroughfare.
  • 露坐 - Sitting exposed to the open air.
  • 戏侮 - Playing pranks or mocking.
  • 堕一网中 - Fell into a net.
  • 投于江流 - Threw into the river current.
  • 无望之获 - An unexpected or unforeseen gain.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 饶州东岳行宫 - Dongyue Palace in Raozhou, a temple dedicated to the Eastern God of Mt. Tai, a significant deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 绍熙五年 - The fifth year of the Shaoxi era, corresponding to 1194 CE in the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 荆南 - Jingnan, a historical region in southern China, now part of Hubei Province.
  • 八渠山 - Baqiu Mountain, a location mentioned in the text.
  • 猢狲 - Monkey, often used metaphorically in Chinese literature.
  • 官券 - Official voucher or document, possibly a form of currency or promise.

Proper Nouns

  • 饶州 - Raozhou, a historical prefecture in Jiangxi Province.
  • 东岳行宫 - Dongyue Palace, a temple dedicated to the Eastern God of Mt. Tai.
  • 荆南 - Jingnan, a historical region in southern China.
  • 八渠山 - Baqiu Mountain, a specific location mentioned in the text.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夢伋着毛衫坐于便齋左右侍直皆呼為知軍 - A dream where Ji is seen wearing a fur coat sitting in the convenience hall, with those around him calling him 'Governor.'
  • 歲未盡十日子靜感疾不起 - Before the end of the year, on the tenth day, Lu Jiuyuan fell ill and did not recover.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 紹興 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song, referring to the period from 1131 to 1162.
  • 荊門 - A city in Hubei province, historically an important military and administrative center.
  • 臨川 - Present-day Nanchang, Jiangxi province, where Lu Jiuyuan was born.
  • 陸九淵 - A prominent Neo-Confucian philosopher, known for his contributions to the development of the 'Mind School' (Xin Xue).
  • 貴溪 - A county in Jiangxi province, where Zhou Li was from.
  • 惠泉亭 - A pavilion in Jingmen, mentioned in Wang Zhan's record.
  • 王瞻叔 - Wang Zhan, a contemporary of Lu Jiuyuan, who wrote the 'Huiquan Pavilion Record.'

Proper Nouns

  • 紹興 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 荊門 - A city in Hubei province.
  • 臨川 - Present-day Nanchang, Jiangxi province.
  • 陸九淵 - A prominent Neo-Confucian philosopher.
  • 貴溪 - A county in Jiangxi province.
  • 惠泉亭 - A pavilion in Jingmen.
  • 王瞻叔 - Wang Zhan, a contemporary of Lu Jiuyuan.
  • 周禮 - The physician from Guixi.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 桂岩 - A place name, likely a mountain or scenic area.
  • 鸷兽 - Refers to a fierce or predatory beast, often used metaphorically.
  • 中夜 - Means 'midnight' or 'deep into the night.'
  • 持炬火前行 - Means 'holding a torch and walking ahead.'
  • 觉有物追随在后 - Means 'feeling that something was following behind.'
  • 两下以矛交刺之 - Means 'both sides thrusting at each other with spears.'
  • 跳登岸 - Means 'jumping onto the shore.'
  • 正值眢井 - Means 'happening to be at a dry well.'
  • 为众撃杀 - Means 'killed by the crowd.'
  • 遍身皆带箭 - Means 'covered with arrows all over the body.'
  • 如小玳瑁筒其表尖锐 - Means 'like small tortoise-shell tubes on the surface, sharp.'
  • 可以治头痒 - Means 'can be used to treat itchy heads.'
  • 寻常与人遇则踈而激之 - Means 'usually when encountering people, it would attack and provoke them.'
  • 或着要害处亦久久方愈 - Means 'if it bites in a vital area, it takes a long time to heal.'
  • 村民程十八者能射虎 - Means 'the villager Cheng Shiba who can shoot tigers.'
  • 里人称为程大虫 - Means 'the villagers call him Cheng Dachong.'
  • 煮草乌头汁以淬箭镞 - Means 'boiling the juice of Aconite and soaking the arrowheads in it.'
  • 施窝机伺于虎出入道上 - Means 'setting traps and waiting on the path where tigers come and go.'
  • 尝有一虎为箭所伤不能行 - Means 'once a tiger was wounded by an arrow and could not move.'
  • 倚树蹲立 - Means 'leaning against a tree and squatting.'
  • 虎死不倒地 - Means 'a dying tiger does not fall to the ground.'
  • 此已死无足惧 - Means 'this one is already dead, there is no need to fear it.'
  • 经前欲取之 - Means 'passing by and wanting to take it.'
  • 虎尚未绝忍痛哮吼 - Means 'the tiger was still alive and roaring in pain.'
  • 举两足来搏 - Means 'raising its two paws to attack.'
  • 程奋臂撑拒 - Means 'Cheng raised his arm to resist.'
  • 力且竭 - Means 'his strength was nearly exhausted.'
  • 其徒望见争奔救 - Means 'his companions saw and rushed to help.'
  • 仅得脱 - Means 'barely managed to escape.'
  • 脑后臂胛背遭爪攫拏 - Means 'his back of the head, arm, and shoulder were grabbed by the tiger's claws.'
  • 卧病几月乃起 - Means 'lying ill for several months before recovering.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 桂岩 - A specific location, possibly a mountain or a scenic area known for its beauty.
  • 鸷兽 - A term often used in classical literature to describe fierce animals, often with symbolic meanings.
  • 程十八 - A specific person, likely a local hero or skilled hunter.
  • 程大虫 - A nickname for Cheng Shiba, possibly derived from his skill in hunting tigers.
  • 草乌头 - A type of poisonous plant used in traditional medicine, particularly for its toxicity.
  • 箭镞 - The tip of an arrow, often made of metal.
  • 窝机 - A type of trap used for hunting.

Proper Nouns

  • 桂岩 - A place name.
  • 程十八 - A person's name.
  • 程大虫 - A nickname for Cheng Shiba.
  • 草乌头 - A specific plant.
  • 箭镞 - A specific part of an arrow.
  • 窝机 - A specific type of trap.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 买我紫罗两疋 - Bought two bolts of purple silk from me.
  • 酬价已足 - Paid the price fully.
  • 寘诸袖间 - Put them in my sleeves.
  • 使我相随取钱 - Made me follow to get the money.
  • 到居宅径入 - Arrived at my residence and went straight in.
  • 今移时已 - Now it has been a while.
  • 愿为一言促之 - Wanted me to speak and hurry him.
  • 吾家欲缣帛何必仗僧为市 - Why do we need a monk to buy silk when we already have it?
  • 且未尝有此徒来往 - Moreover, we have never had such people come and go.
  • 汝亟去 - You should leave quickly.
  • 客不肯去力言之 - The guest refused to leave and spoke forcefully.
  • 仍述其形貌及袖衣穿弊之状 - Still described his appearance and the worn-out state of his sleeve.
  • 璋始悟 - Zhan finally realized.
  • 亟往所事泗州菩萨堂验视 - Rushed to the temple where he worshipped the Bodhisattva in Suzhou to check.
  • 则两缣正在左侧 - And the two bolts of silk were indeed on the left side.
  • 盖常时崇奉甚敬被以真服 - Because he usually revered and respected it, dressing it in a true robe.
  • 未暇更新是日为之瞿然 - Not having time to replace it, on that day, he was startled.
  • 尽室焚香谢过 - Burned incense and apologized to the whole family.
  • 取钱以偿客 - Took the money to compensate the guest.
  • 而唤匠治衣易之 - And called a craftsman to make new clothes for it.
  • 喻氏招医 - Inviting a doctor.
  • 鄱阳士人汪櫄居于郭外数十里间 - Wang Chun, a scholar from Poyang, lived dozens of miles outside the city.
  • 妻喻氏以绍熈五年初秋感疾 - His wife, Yu, fell ill in the early autumn of the fifth year of Shaoxi.
  • 伏枕两旬更数医治疗弗效 - Laid on the bed for two weeks, treated by several doctors without success.
  • 其家议欲招刘昶 - The family discussed wanting to invite Liu Chang.
  • 尝为江东提刑司缉捕官 - He once served as a detective officer in the Jiangdong Criminal Court.
  • 因寓处城中 - Because he lived in the city.
  • 是夜櫄梦一异人授以金刀 - That night, Chun dreamed of a strange man giving him a golden knife.
  • 光彩粲然长径尺 - With brilliant light, about a foot long.
  • 觉而黙喜自念金刀者刘也 - Awakening, he silently rejoiced, thinking the golden knife was Liu.
  • 当符于昶无所疑 - It must be a sign for Chang, without any doubt.
  • 夙兴命仆且行 - Early in the morning, he ordered his servant to go.
  • 方啓门而其友宋震适至 - Just as he was opening the door, his friend Song Zhen happened to arrive.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '抚州宜黄县宰徐君听讼' introduces the magistrate and his role in hearing lawsuits. The structure is straightforward but requires clear identification of the magistrate and his duties.
  • The long sentence '侯林哀哭陈牒云居于社坛之旁遭弓手夏生纵火焚荡所居遂并三子为灰烬' describes the plaintiff's situation and the alleged crime. It can be broken down into smaller, more manageable parts to ensure clarity.
  • The sequence '阅词骇怆即捕夏送狱讯鞫甚苦夏不知所对泣涕弗食' describes the magistrate's reaction and the interrogation of Xia. The emotional and procedural aspects need to be clearly conveyed.
  • The complex sentence '县吏共言夏为人素循理安得有白昼放火杀人之事愿追词首究其末仍委佐官往本处验实当可得情' involves multiple actions and opinions. It should be broken down to maintain logical flow and clarity.
  • The final sequence '徐用其说及诣坛下物色并无侯老住址与火炀之迹乃夏生以祀社' describes the magistrate's actions and the outcome of the investigation. The logical progression of events needs to be clearly articulated.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '荆门长林县民蹇大居郭北七八十里间有一女纳同里邹亚刘为赘婿' introduces the main characters and their relationships, but it is quite long and complex.

  • The clause '据其屋耕其田又致诸死地而掩取其产' describes the husband's actions against his wife's family, but it is also complex.
  • The clause '数至蹇氏浸浸与女通' describes the visitor's frequent visits and growing intimacy with the daughter, but it is also complex.
  • The clause '苟能杀邹郎以女嫁汝' contains a conditional statement, which can be ambiguous in its exact meaning.
  • The clause '急呼语李使持刃出迎之' describes the husband's urgent call to his accomplice, but it is also complex.
  • The clause '才相值奋斫贠背坠马死继又戕邹' describes the sequence of events leading to the murders, but it is also complex.
  • The clause '适无人行后乃稍稍集会仓卒之际莫知凶变所起' describes the aftermath of the murders, but it is also complex.

  • 予和之云海树凌根石効竒山经地志不曽知 - Means 'I responded with a poem saying that the sea tree, rooted in stone, is a wonder that has never been recorded in mountain books or geographical records,' emphasizing the uniqueness of the stone pine.
  • 凝丹幻出珠千颗染紫装成玉一枝 - Means 'the refined elixir has created thousands of pearls, dyed purple and formed into a jade branch,' describing the appearance of the stone pine.
  • 骨龙姿随质见鸾栖鳯宿与香宜 - Means 'the dragon-like posture of the bones, seen according to the substance, is suitable for the roosting of cranes and phoenixes, and is fragrant,' describing the beauty and fragrance of the stone pine.
  • 元戎髙唱真难和愧我年来笔力衰 - Means 'the general's high singing is truly difficult to match, I am ashamed of my declining pen skills in recent years,' expressing humility and admiration.
  • 又有石梅石松则未之见也 - Means 'there are also stone plum and stone pine, which I have not seen,' indicating the rarity of these formations.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宣和中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Huizong of Song, specifically the year 1123 (the third year of the Xuanhe era).
  • 成都士曺 - Refers to the office of the prefect of Chengdu. '士曺' is an archaic term for the prefectural administration.
  • 母冀夫人 - Refers to Zhang Jun's mother, Lady Ji. '夫人' is a respectful title for a high-ranking official's wife.
  • 奉道 - Means 'to follow the Tao' or 'to practice Taoism.'
  • 每日齋道人一員 - Refers to the practice of offering a Taoist practitioner a vegetarian meal daily. '齋' means a vegetarian meal or a ritual offering.
  • 或親焚香加禮 - Means 'sometimes personally burning incense and adding extra respect.'
  • 有貴子 - Means 'a noble or distinguished son.'
  • 今安在 - Means 'where is he now?'
  • 見為曺官方在州倉納粟 - Refers to Zhang Jun's current position as the prefect of Chengdu, where he is overseeing grain storage.
  • 亟趨出 - Means 'quickly left' or 'hurriedly departed.'
  • 不暇扣其何處人何姓氏也 - Means 'did not have time to ask about his place of origin or name.'
  • 所言既驗 - Means 'what was said proved to be true.'
  • 陰訪求之 - Means 'to secretly seek him out.'
  • 莫能得 - Means 'could not find him.'
  • 谪居和州 - Refers to Zhang Jun being demoted to live in Hezhou.
  • 为秦丞相所忌 - Means 'was disliked by the Chancellor of Qin.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 張魏公 - Zhang Jun (1091–1164), a statesman and general during the Southern Song dynasty, known for his efforts to resist the Jin dynasty.
  • 宣和 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song (1119–1125), a period marked by cultural flourishing but also political decline.
  • 成都 - The capital of Sichuan province, historically an important cultural and economic center.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 地公絶憂之未知所以自脱 - Literal translation: 'Lord Earth has been in extreme distress, not knowing how to extricate himself.' Conveys deep anxiety and helplessness.
  • 忽排闼而入 - Literal translation: 'Suddenly burst in through the door.' Indicates a dramatic and unexpected arrival.
  • 望其仪貎俨与三十年前无少异 - Literal translation: 'Looking at his appearance, he was just as dignified as he was thirty years ago.' Suggests a remarkable lack of change over time.
  • 迎语之曰 - Literal translation: 'He greeted him and said.' Introduces a direct quote.
  • 吾昔求先生不可致 - Literal translation: 'I once sought you but could not find you.' Indicates a past unsuccessful search.
  • 正尔牢落 - Literal translation: 'You are just as desolate.' Expresses a sense of loneliness or isolation.
  • 肯恵然相顾何邪 - Literal translation: 'Would you kindly look after me? Why?' A polite and somewhat rhetorical question.
  • 知公以时相为忧 - Literal translation: 'Knowing that you are worried about your situation.' Acknowledges the subject's concerns.
  • 彼乃死人耳 - Literal translation: 'He is just a dead person.' A blunt statement suggesting that the concern is unfounded.
  • 何足虑 - Literal translation: 'What is there to worry about?' A dismissive response.
  • 异时公当复旧物 - Literal translation: 'In the future, you will regain your former possessions.' A promise of restoration.
  • 福未艾也 - Literal translation: 'Your good fortune is not yet exhausted.' Indicates that better times are ahead.
  • 揖客 - Literal translation: 'He bowed to the guest.' A gesture of respect.
  • 熟睨良久 - Literal translation: 'He looked at him for a long time.' Indicates a prolonged and careful observation.
  • 两眼视物欲裂 - Literal translation: 'His eyes were about to burst from looking at things.' Suggests intense focus or strain.
  • 好处正在阿堵中 - Literal translation: 'The good fortune is right here.' A colloquial expression pointing to the present moment.
  • 他日为西南帅臣 - Literal translation: 'In the future, he will become a military governor in the southwest.' A prediction of success.
  • 名满天下 - Literal translation: 'Famous throughout the world.' Indicates widespread recognition.
  • 方数岁 - Literal translation: 'He was just a few years old.' Indicates youth.
  • 在寝未起 - Literal translation: 'He was still asleep in bed.' Suggesting inactivity.
  • 尚有稚儿欲丐题品 - Literal translation: 'There is still a young child whom I wish to ask for your opinion.' A request for evaluation.
  • 但正睡熟不可唤觉 - Literal translation: 'But he is sleeping soundly and cannot be woken up.' Indicates a state of deep sleep.
  • 秉烛就视之 - Literal translation: 'He took a candle and went to look at him.' Describes a specific action.
  • 笑曰 - Literal translation: 'He laughed and said.' Introduces a direct quote.
  • 大有福胜如哥哥 - Literal translation: 'He has great fortune, even surpassing his older brother.' A comparison of fortune.
  • 未易量也 - Literal translation: 'It is not easy to measure.' Suggesting that the child's potential is difficult to assess.
  • 少顷告去讫不复再见 - Literal translation: 'After a short while, he left and did not return again.' Indicates a final departure.
  • 应山槐 - Literal translation: 'The槐 of Yingshan.' A specific reference to a tree in Yingshan.
  • 过小溪有野寺 - Literal translation: 'After crossing a small stream, there is a wild temple.' Describes a physical setting.
  • 寺外数十步间一槐 - Literal translation: 'A槐 tree stands between the temple and the surrounding area.' Indicates a specific location.
  • 根株与常槐等 - Literal translation: 'Its roots and trunk are similar to those of ordinary槐 trees.' Indicates a comparison.
  • 遍体柔条结若藤蔓然 - Literal translation: 'Its entire body is covered with soft, intertwined branches like vines.' Describes the tree's appearance.
  • 莫见其所起处 - Literal translation: 'No one can see where it originated.' Suggests a mysterious origin.
  • 每枝必分两岐 - Literal translation: 'Each branch splits into two.' Describes the tree's branching pattern.
  • 叶叶皆背面而生 - Literal translation: 'Each leaf grows on the back side.' Indicates a unique growth pattern.
  • 无一相对 - Literal translation: 'None are opposite each other.' Emphasizes the asymmetry.
  • 虽孙枝数寸者亦然 - Literal translation: 'Even the smallest branches are the same.' Indicates consistency.
  • 婆娑茂蔚 - Literal translation: 'Graceful and lush.' Describes the tree's overall appearance.
  • 土民相与爱护 - Literal translation: 'The local people love and protect it.' Indicates community care.
  • 故露植空旷之野 - Literal translation: 'Therefore, it is planted in the open, uncultivated fields.' Describes the tree's environment.
  • 未尝翦伐 - Literal translation: 'It has never been pruned or cut down.' Indicates preservation.
  • 士大夫经此必往赏翫 - Literal translation: 'Scholars and officials always stop to admire it.' Indicates its cultural significance.
  • 政和中花石之役兴 - Literal translation: 'During the Huashi Campaign in the Zhenghe era.' Refers to a specific historical event.
  • 有欲徙置禁苑者 - Literal translation: 'There were those who wanted to move it to the imperial garden.' Indicates an attempt to relocate the tree.
  • 而盘踞牢甚不可发掘 - Literal translation: 'But it was so firmly rooted that it could not be dug up.' Describes the tree's stability.
  • 乃止 - Literal translation: 'So they gave up.' Indicates the failure of the attempt.
  • 鄱阳张焄为邑宰 - Literal translation: 'Zhang Xun of Poyang was the county magistrate.' Introduces a historical figure.
  • 命画师睥睨写为图 - Literal translation: 'He ordered a painter to sketch it.' Describes an artistic representation.
  • 以寄饷四远 - Literal translation: 'To send to people far and wide.' Indicates distribution.
  • 竟不能落笔 - Literal translation: 'But he could not put it on paper.' Suggests a failure in the artistic endeavor.
  • 亦止 - Literal translation: 'So he gave up.' Indicates the end of the attempt.

  • 夏四月邻火延烧所积一空明日泣理故处于烬中得故缾畧无壊缺而钱自镕成寳像髙一尺许冠衣璎珞杨枝浄缾皆具工制妙巧塑匠惊叹以为不能及巨室王氏取去营一室严奉留媪事香火夀过百嵗绍熙中犹存顷呉斗南书明州民媪一事全相似已载庚志中佛力不可思议普欲示化不嫌于同也 - Means 'In the fourth month of summer, a neighbor's fire spread and burned everything she had saved to nothing. The next day, she cried as she sorted through the ashes and found the old vase, which was slightly damaged but the money inside had melted into a treasure image of Guanyin, about a foot tall, with a crown, clothing, jewelry, and a willow branch and a clean vase, all exquisitely crafted. The sculptor was amazed and said he could not match it. A wealthy family named Wang took it away, built a room to worship it, and left the old woman to manage the incense and candles. She lived to be over a hundred years old, and the treasure image still existed during the Shaoxi era. Recently, Wu Dounan wrote about a similar story of a common woman in Mingzhou, which is fully recorded in the *Geng Zhi*. The power of the Buddha is beyond imagination, and it does not hesitate to show its transformation, even if the stories are similar.'

  • 防行过深夜 - Indicates the time of day, specifically the late night, when the journey is taking place.
  • 埃翳颇散稍见明月 - Describes the gradual clearing of dust and mist, allowing the moon to become visible.
  • 若虎啸声急置担仗与二仆升髙木自缚移时乃下 - Describes the urgency of the situation, where the travelers quickly place their burdens and climb a high tree, tying themselves up for safety.
  • 复进前得一草舍翁媪出揖惊贺曰 - Describes the travelers finding a small thatched house and being greeted by an elderly couple.
  • 官人定有后福忆所过挂纸钱处乎 - A greeting and a question asking if the traveler remembers passing a place where paper money was hung.
  • 魑魅防两白昼牵人衣领上下又多猛虎岂容平行安步畧无困恼真可贺也 - Describes the dangers encountered, including spirits and tigers, and the relief of not being harmed.
  • 他日归至南安则薛忠辈既事败就擒戮于市矣 - Predicts the future fate of the criminals.
  • 处恭字安礼工为诗予前志书之后锡山士人陈善为记其事以为姜平生为人行义孝友故値凶盗行区蹈虎境履危如此皆获免云 - Provides additional information about the person and the reason for their survival.
  • 琴台棋桌 - Introduces the topic of a musical platform and a chess table.
  • 乾道初内侍陈源坐罪谪郴诏籍其家赀出鬻作吕丞义卿得二物其一琴台虢州月石所斵色紫而理细茂林脩竹江村小景工妙不可名状四脚各以绿丝绦系降真香靸子一枚其一象棋桌髙一尺五寸阔二尺五寸空中以贮棋合四围有防沈香为局面牙栅界之其外用乌木花黎白檀数匝缘饰以降真香刻水浪加金填而 - Describes the acquisition and characteristics of the musical platform and chess table.

  • 降真香 - A type of incense.
  • 乌木花黎白檀 - Types of wood.
  • 沈香 - Agarwood, a fragrant wood.

  • The phrase '雾翳合对面不分谁何防行过深夜' is a compound sentence describing the dense fog and the late-night journey.
  • The phrase '埃翳颇散稍见明月傍侧若虎啸声急置担仗与二仆升髙木自缚移时乃下' is a complex sentence describing the travelers' actions in response to the danger.

  • The phrase '官人定有后福忆所过挂纸钱处乎' is a compound sentence combining a greeting and a question.
  • The phrase '魑魅防两白昼牵人衣领上下又多猛虎岂容平行安步畧无困恼真可贺也' is a compound sentence describing the dangers and the relief of not being harmed.
  • The phrase '他日归至南安则薛忠辈既事败就擒戮于市矣' is a conditional sentence predicting the future fate of the criminals.
  • The phrase '处恭字安礼工为诗予前志书之后锡山士人陈善为记其事以为姜平生为人行义孝友故値凶盗行区蹈虎境履危如此皆获免云' is a complex sentence providing additional information about the person and the reason for their survival.
  • The phrase '琴台棋桌' is a simple introduction to the topic.
  • The phrase '乾道初内侍陈源坐罪谪郴诏籍其家赀出鬻作吕丞义卿得二物其一琴台虢州月石所斵色紫而理细茂林脩竹江村小景工妙不可名状四脚各以绿丝绦系降真香靸子一枚其一象棋桌髙一尺五寸阔二尺五寸空中以贮棋合四围有防沈香为局面牙栅界之其外用乌木花黎白檀数匝缘饰以降真香刻水浪加金填而' is a complex sentence describing the acquisition and characteristics of the musical platform and chess table.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 浪头填以银芬芗袭人他物一切称是 - Describes a luxurious and opulent setting with '浪头' (waves) metaphorically representing lavish decorations, '银芬' (silver fragrance), and '芗袭人' (aromatics pervading the air). '他物一切称是' means that all other items are in proportion.
  • 其居在秘书省东连墙起楼楼下筑台 - Describes the location and structure of a residence, with '秘书省' (Ministry of Rites or Imperial Library) indicating a prestigious location.
  • 每延宴客张乐必于其处 - '延宴客' means to invite guests for a banquet, and '张乐' means to play music.
  • 有闽士献书警之曰 - '献书' means to present a written warning or advice.
  • 宅西正是三馆职多穷寒措大羡人富贵于心常以弗堪稍迁则为台谏给舎或能害尔 - A complex sentence describing a warning about the dangers of envying wealth and power, with '三馆' (three halls) referring to the imperial libraries, '穷寒措大' (poor and cold officials), and '台谏给舎' (high-ranking officials).
  • 是后遂不复至此书杂于故纸中故外间得见之 - '是后' (after this) and '故外间得见之' (so it was seen by outsiders) indicate the fate of the warning.
  • 貂珰僣侈之极罪不可胜诛尚尔漏网 - '貂珰' (douzhang, officials with high ranks), '僣侈' (extravagance), and '漏网' (escape punishment) describe the extreme luxury and its consequences.
  • 吕氏绿毛 - '绿毛' (green-scaled turtle) refers to a specific type of turtle.
  • 吕徳卿家畜一绿毛于盆池中久而甚驯 - '家畜' (keep at home), '盆池' (pond), and '驯' (tame) describe the turtle's domestication.
  • 每至日午以小竹杖击水面必应声而出即就杖头插生猪肉数小脔饲之食尽复入 - '日午' (noon), '小竹杖' (small bamboo stick), '生猪肉' (raw pork), and '小脔' (small pieces) describe the turtle's feeding routine.
  • 稚儿欲为戏用此时击水出而取之寘于定盆次日归之至午不复出经六七日使童子没臂求索则死矣 - '稚儿' (young child), '定盆' (fixed bowl), and '没臂' (dip arm) describe the turtle's death.
  • 此一介虫之防愠于失信宁不食而死异哉 - '一介虫' (a small creature), '防愠' (resentment), and '异哉' (strange indeed) describe the turtle's reaction to betrayal.
  • 又鲁子礼书室石斛中育白仅若小钱大背文皆黄金色有光采双目如漆后因以鰕醢与食金文脱落而毙 - '石斛' (stone pot), '白' (white turtle), '金文' (golden script), and '鰕醢' (shrimp sauce) describe the turtle's death due to poisoning.
  • 秀州市民社会有一若药瓢然询其人云自始得时以铁卷束其腹背故如此 - '药瓢' (medicine ladle), '铁卷' (iron scroll), and '束其腹背' (bind its belly and back) describe the turtle's condition.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建康歸正官王和尚 - Refers to Wang Haoshi, an official from Jiankang who has returned to the fold (歸正). '建康' is the former name of Nanjing, and '歸正' suggests a return to a proper or official status.
  • 能誦完顔亮小詞 - Means 'able to recite the small poems (詞) of Wang Anshi.' '小詞' refers to short lyrical poems, often of a personal or intimate nature.
  • 其詠雪昭君怨曰 - Indicates that the following is a poem titled '昭君怨' (Zhaohun Yuan) that Wang Anshi composed about snow.
  • 驚問是楊花是蘆花 - A rhetorical question asking whether the falling snow is willow catkins (楊花) or reed flowers (蘆花), both of which can resemble snow.
  • 比夜看姮娥體態 - Refers to the moon goddess Chang'e (姮娥), suggesting that the poet is comparing the moon's appearance to her graceful form.
  • 寳積行者 - Refers to a monk named Baogong, who is known for his spiritual practices and miracles.
  • 台州仙居縣寳積寺 - Refers to Baogong Temple in Xianju County, Taizhou, Zhejiang Province.
  • 雖小刹而従來規矩整嚴 - Indicates that despite being a small temple, it has always maintained strict discipline and order.
  • 僧圎悟主寺事 - Refers to Master Yuanwu, who is the abbot of the temple.
  • 園人陳甲常種蔬菜來鬻直堂 - Describes a gardener named Chen Jia, who often brings vegetables to sell in the temple.
  • 行者慶脩竊其一畦 - Indicates that a monk named Qinxiu secretly stole a plot of Chen Jia's vegetables.
  • 陳妻王氏知之而不克與競 - Indicates that Chen Jia's wife, Wang, knew about the theft but could not confront the monk.
  • 但仰空詛云 - Describes Wang's act of cursing the monk by looking up to the sky and speaking.
  • 我所失菜直一貫二百錢 - Specifies the amount of money lost due to the theft.
  • 數你做畜生還我 - A curse directed at the monk, calling him an animal and demanding repayment.
  • 已而慶脩病傷寒 - Indicates that Qinxiu later fell ill with a severe fever.
  • 奄奄防一嵗 - Describes Qinxiu's prolonged illness, lasting for a year.
  • 向來實不合取陳家菜 - Admits that he was wrong to take Chen Jia's vegetables.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 人来云王大伯叫汝即以黒衣加我体 — Someone came and said that Master Wang called you, and immediately put on black clothes on my body.
  • 遂被驱入其舍后先已有七八人在彼矣 — And was then driven into the back of his house, where there were already seven or eight people.
  • 约月余复独挈我至陈园顾视自身乃成一猪 — After about a month, he took me alone to Chen Garden, and when I looked at myself, I had become a pig.
  • 窘急思归而为絷缚 — I was in a desperate state, wanting to return home, but was bound.
  • 以糟食喂饲我数月后肌肤充腯 — After feeding me with fermented rice for several months, my skin and muscles became full and plump.
  • 特货于张屠正得钱千二百 — He specially sold me to Zhang the butcher and got 1200 coins.
  • 施刀猛刺喉下痛不可忍 — He applied the knife and stabbed fiercely under my throat, the pain was unbearable.
  • 大叫一声欻如梦觉自是疾瘳 — I screamed once and suddenly woke up like from a dream, from then on, I quickly recovered.
  • 徐以其事询诸人所説皆同 — Gradually, I asked people about the matter, and what they said was all the same.
  • 记中载死而偿债多矣 — The records say that many have died to repay their debts.
  • 若庆脩生而为猪未之有也 — If someone like Qingsheng was born as a pig, it has never happened before.
  • 紫槀长老清可亲见之 — The elder Qing, who lived in Ziqiao, personally witnessed it.
  • 何左司 — He the Left Censor.
  • 何万一之陈谔蹇叔皆福州名士 — He Wan Yi, Chen E, and Jian Shu, all from Fuzhou, were famous scholars.
  • 平生最相厚善 — They were the closest and most virtuous friends in life.
  • 虽为乡里交游而情好不异骨肉 — Although they were friends from the same hometown, their affection was no different from that of siblings.
  • 淳熙十三年同为宰属 — In the thirteenth year of Chongxi, they were both officials.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 家人梦先亡祖考尽集云 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous and can be interpreted as 'family members dream that all the deceased ancestors gather and say...' or 'family members dream that all the deceased ancestors gather.'
  • 因小七不改故已十余次作猪死于其手 - The phrase '因小七不改' can be interpreted as 'because Xiao Qi does not change' or 'because Xiao Qi does not repent.'
  • 圈中所蓄总是我辈 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'the pigs in the pen are all of our kind' or 'the pigs in the pen are all of us.'
  • 还债已足切勿更加杀害 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'the debt is paid, do not kill any more' or 'the debt is paid, do not harm any more.'
  • 至旦往视之 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'by morning, go to see it' or 'by morning, go to observe it.'
  • 群豕首悉变为人 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'the heads of the pigs all turn into human form' or 'the heads of the pigs all become human.'
  • 若祖若父若伯叔若姑姊妹 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'like grandfather, father, uncles, aunts, sisters' or 'like ancestors, fathers, uncles, aunts, sisters.'
  • 悲酸出言大略与昨梦所告等 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'they speak with sorrow, roughly as told in yesterday's dream' or 'they speak with sorrow, similar to what was told in yesterday's dream.'
  • 而下体固畜类也 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'but the lower body remains that of an animal' or 'but the lower body is still that of an animal.'
  • 顷之复故 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'soon after, they return to their original form' or 'soon after, they revert to their original state.'
  • 外人无所见 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'others do not see this' or 'others do not witness this.'
  • 童遽空群舍入寺且饭僧懴佛 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'Tong immediately leaves the pig pen, goes to the temple, and offers food to the monks while repenting' or 'Tong quickly leaves the pig pen, goes to the temple, and offers food to the monks while confessing.'
  • 痛自洗悔而改货纱帛以自给 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'he deeply repents and changes his livelihood to selling silk and cloth' or 'he deeply repents and changes his trade to selling silk and cloth.'
  • 至今犹存 - This phrase can be interpreted as 'he still exists to this day' or 'he still lives to this day.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不暇挈家 - '不暇' means no time or opportunity, '挈家' means to bring one's family; the phrase means 'did not have time to bring their families.'
  • 日久憂惱 - '日久' means over time, '憂惱' means worry or anxiety; the phrase means 'after a long time, he became worried and distressed.'
  • 行滅跡法 - Reference to a mystical or magical practice, possibly related to divination or exorcism; '滅跡' could mean to erase or remove traces, '法' is a method or technique.
  • 通靈能預知未來事 - '通靈' means to communicate with spirits or supernatural beings, '預知未來事' means to foresee future events; this is a common description of a shaman or spirit medium.
  • 方降即云 - '方降' means just as she descended or appeared, '即云' means immediately said; the phrase is a narrative construction used to describe the action of the shaman speaking.
  • 厚致錢往謝一切弗受 - '厚致錢' means to give a generous amount of money, '往謝' means to go and thank, '一切弗受' means she refused everything; this is a common pattern in classical Chinese narratives about miraculous or spiritual figures.
  • 每為客道此 - '為客道此' means 'often told this to guests'; this is a narrative technique used to show that the story was recounted by others.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '南岳庙火诏潭州重修命湘潭令薛大主督役' is a series of actions compressed into a single clause. It should be parsed as: 'The fire at the Nan Yue Temple led to an imperial edict ordering the governor of Tan Prefecture to rebuild it, and the magistrate of Xiangtan, Xue Dazhu, was appointed to oversee the construction.'
  • The phrase '深山穷谷求取殆遍' can be read as 'almost every deep mountain and remote valley was searched,' but the word order is somewhat ambiguous.
  • The phrase '正殿缺一梁当长五丈而径五六尺者' describes the missing beam but is somewhat elliptical. A more complete rendering would be 'the main hall was missing a beam that was five zhang long and five or six chi in diameter.'
  • The phrase '中度搜访不可得' means 'after a thorough search, it could not be found,' but the word order is somewhat ambiguous.
  • The phrase '或言湘潭境内黄冈白马大王庙前有巨杉' introduces a rumor, and the structure is somewhat elliptical. A more complete rendering would be 'someone said there was a giant cedar tree in front of the Temple of the Great King of Yellow Ridge and White Horse in Xiangtan.'
  • The phrase '今集事非此木不可' means 'now that the project is underway, this tree is indispensable,' but the word order is somewhat ambiguous.
  • The phrase '但庙神灵威怖人那复敢近' means 'but the temple's divine spirit is terrifying to people, who would dare approach it?' but the structure is somewhat elliptical.
  • The phrase '薛审访得实即具文牒遣邑尉王以宁焚于庙下' describes a series of actions compressed into a single clause. It should be parsed as: 'Xue verified the information and found it to be true, so he immediately prepared a document and sent the county magistrate Wang Yining to burn it at the temple.'
  • The phrase '然后领工匠百辈厉斤斧伐木至其傍' describes a series of actions compressed into a single clause. It should be parsed as: 'then he led a hundred craftsmen with axes to chop down the tree.'
  • The phrase '见长蛇蟠踞根株匠手颤股栗拱立相视' describes the craftsmen's reaction to the snake, but the structure is somewhat elliptical. A more complete rendering would be 'they saw a long snake coiled around the roots, and the craftsmen trembled, their legs shaking, and stood in awe.'
  • The phrase '尉遣信驰报薛薛防马亲诣庙致牲牢酒醴敬祷' describes a series of actions compressed into a single clause. It should be parsed as: 'the magistrate sent a messenger to report to Xue, and Xue rode a horse and personally went to the temple to offer sacrifices and wine, praying respectfully.'
  • The phrase '此方壤地皆岳帝所司' means 'this land is under the jurisdiction of the Lord of the Southern Peak.'
  • The phrase '今崇建宫宇出于制敕' means 'now the construction of the palace is ordered by imperial decree.'
  • The phrase '区区一木当以为栋梁之献' means 'this small tree should be offered as a beam for the palace.'
  • The phrase '尚何爱惜哉' means 'why should we be so attached to it?'
  • The phrase '取杯珓掷之得吉卜' means 'he threw the divination cups and got a favorable result.'
  • The phrase '于是百斧并进闻杪锵然如剑防声' means 'then a hundred axes were swung, and the sound of the tree's top falling was like the clashing of swords.'
  • The phrase '匠惧欲止薛不聴' means 'the craftsmen were afraid and wanted to stop, but Xue did not listen.'
  • The phrase '明日树根汁出如血迸流满地' means 'the next day, the tree's sap flowed like blood, covering the ground.'
  • The phrase '大声如雷既断而盘空旋绕欲堕不堕' means 'a loud sound like thunder, and the tree trunk, once cut, spun in the air, wanting to fall but not falling.'
  • The phrase '薛麾众趋避复白神云' means 'Xue waved the crowd to move away and then reported to the spirit.'
  • The phrase '既已许我愿勿以光响灵异动人' means 'since you have already allowed us, please do not use light, sound, or supernatural phenomena to frighten us.'
  • The phrase '如是者逾时方仆' means 'after a while, the tree finally fell.'
  • The phrase '还谒庙奠谢神像遍体悉拆裂' means 'he returned to the temple to offer sacrifices and thank the spirit, whose image was completely split apart.'
  • The phrase '观者落胆' means 'the onlookers were terrified.'
  • The phrase '蛇是其精魂云' means 'the tree snake was its spirit.'

  • 既而平愈 - Subsequently recovered or he soon recovered; '平' suggests a return to normalcy.

  • 不可杀于堤岸 - Should not be killed near dikes or embankments; a warning in traditional Chinese ecological or folk wisdom.
  • 透地脉 - Penetrate the earth's veins; metaphorical expression for the animal's ability to affect the earth's structure.
  • 宿恙暂作 - A long-term illness temporarily flared up; '宿恙' refers to a chronic illness.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes Zhu Xian's long-term service and character: 'Zhu Xian served my family as a horseman for twenty years, being diligent and cautious without fault.'
  • This sentence describes Zhu Xian's sudden action: 'When he arrived at White Deer Ridge on the 26th day of the month, during the daytime, he suddenly bowed his hands three times to the left.'
  • This sentence describes the reaction of his companions: 'His companions asked him why he did that, and he said he saw three or four officials sitting together but did not greet them, and there was nothing to be seen there.'
  • This sentence describes Zhu Xian's condition and death: 'Just then, they suspected it was a vague and mysterious state, and shortly after, he lowered his head, cried out in pain, and could barely be supported to the inn. By then, he could not speak and died that night.'
  • This sentence describes the villagers' suspicion: 'The locals said that where Zhu Xian bowed his hands, there was an old small temple, and they suspected that the ghost was causing harm.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以决胜负 - Means 'to determine victory or defeat,' often used in the context of competitions or contests.
  • 佥以为然 - Means 'all agreed that it was so,' indicating unanimous agreement.
  • 望见为如前可欺 - Means 'saw them as before and could be deceived,' suggesting that the dogs appeared as they did in previous encounters.
  • 群吠而至 - Means 'barked collectively and rushed forward,' describing the behavior of the dogs.
  • 奋迎之势若猛虎之视羊豕 - Means 'with a vigorous attack, like a tiger facing sheep and pigs,' vividly depicting the aggressive stance of the dogs.
  • 或绝其咽或破其胁 - Means 'some choked them, others broke their ribs,' detailing the violent actions of the dogs.
  • 皆立死 - Means 'all died immediately,' emphasizing the swift and brutal nature of the outcome.
  • 凡杀四五犬 - Means 'killed a total of four or five dogs,' providing a specific count of the casualties.
  • 余悉奔遁 - Means 'the rest all fled,' indicating the retreat of the surviving dogs.
  • 众卒烹食死者肉 - Means 'the soldiers cooked and ate the meat of the dead dogs,' describing the aftermath of the fight.
  • 厌饫之外复归遗妻子 - Means 'after being satisfied, they returned home to give the leftovers to their wives and children,' showing the practical use of the meat.
  • 经旬日顾念得肉之利 - Means 'after ten days, they recalled the benefit of the meat,' reflecting on the value gained from the fight.
  • 俄有两虎厖颇大出不意而至 - Means 'soon, two large and fierce tigers unexpectedly arrived,' describing a sudden and dangerous event.
  • 少焉客主各殒其一 - Means 'after a while, both the guest and the host lost one of their tigers,' describing the outcome of the encounter.
  • 存者流血呻呼而散 - Means 'the surviving tigers bled and cried out, then scattered,' showing the aftermath of the fight.
  • 不越夕并死 - Means 'they did not survive the night and both died,' indicating the final outcome.
  • 行林院鸬鷀 - Refers to the story of the heron at the Xinglin Temple.
  • 洪府奉新县之东三十里有僧舍曰行林院 - Means 'Thirty li east of Fuxin County in Hongfu Prefecture, there is a temple called Xinglin Temple.'
  • 院有松冈巨松参天 - Means 'The temple has a pine hill with tall pine trees reaching the sky,' describing the setting.
  • 禽鸟群栖其上鸬鷀最多 - Means 'Birds gather in flocks on top of it, and herons are the most numerous,' highlighting the presence of herons.
  • 每岁字育及秋乃去 - Means 'Each year, they raise their young, and by autumn, they leave,' describing the herons' seasonal behavior.
  • 积十数年罹其虐者以千计 - Means 'Over ten years, thousands of people suffered from his cruelty,' indicating the extent of his actions.
  • 绍熙甲寅夏率其徒至松下系小笯于间攀挟乔枝履虚而上将及木杪老鸬鷀在焉悲噪苦切已而羣飞竞集绕王生之身啄其股攫其目 - Means 'In the summer of Shaoxi Jia Yin, he led his followers to the pine trees below, tied a small cage to a branch, climbed up, and reached the top where an old heron was, which cried bitterly. Soon, the herons flew together and surrounded Wang Liu, pecking his legs and grabbing his eyes,' describing the confrontation with the herons.
  • 王尽力挟松两手皆不可释其徒仰视之争呼曰勿取雏且亟下未能及半啄攫者犹不舍遂颠坠死举体如斧斨然 - Means 'Wang Liu tried his best to hold onto the pine tree, but his hands could not let go. His followers looked up and shouted, 'Don't take the chicks, and hurry down.' But before he reached halfway, the herons still wouldn't let go, and he fell and died, his body looking like an axe,' describing the tragic end of Wang Liu.

  • 众惧莫敢逼 - Everyone was afraid and dared not approach.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 名累及亲族 - Means 'the name of the person involved brings disgrace to their relatives.' Common expression in Classical Chinese describing the negative impact of someone's actions on their family.
  • 宣望之甚怒 - Means 'Xuan was very angry.' '望之' conveys a sense of anger or displeasure.
  • 必取之 - Means 'I must take it.' '必' emphasizes the necessity or determination.
  • 率众趋下 - Means 'led the troops down.' '趋下' can be translated as 'to move quickly downward' or 'to lead the troops down.'
  • 移晷 - Means 'a long time' or 'a considerable period.' '晷' refers to the shadow of a sundial, symbolizing the passage of time.
  • 翻为所败 - Means 'was instead defeated.' '翻' means 'instead' or 'unexpectedly,' and '为所败' means 'was defeated.'
  • 宣与二兵得三级 - Means 'Xuan and two soldiers captured three levels.' '三级' likely refers to three ranks or positions.
  • 驰取径路 - Means 'rushed to take the shortcut.' '驰取' means 'to rush to take,' and '径路' means 'shortcut' or 'direct path.'
  • 转而西方 - Means 'turned westward.' '转' means 'to turn,' and '西方' means 'west.'
  • 穿槀桔中陷于淖 - Means 'penetrated through the dried reeds and fell into the mud.' '穿槀桔' means 'penetrated through the dried reeds,' and '陷于淖' means 'fell into the mud.'
  • 盗追及俱遭屠脔 - Means 'the bandits caught up with them and all were slaughtered.' '盗追及' means 'the bandits caught up with,' and '俱遭屠脔' means 'all were slaughtered.'
  • 二犬犹存 - Means 'the two dogs remained alive.' '犹存' means 'still remained.'
  • 守其尸 - Means 'guarded their bodies.' '守' means 'to guard' or 'to protect.'
  • 保伍环集以事白于县 - Means 'the neighborhood gathered around to report the matter to the county.' '保伍' refers to the neighborhood, '环集' means 'gathered around,' and '以事白于县' means 'reported the matter to the county.'
  • 元佐回邑厚恤三家 - Means 'the county official returned to the town and generously comforted the three families.' '元佐' refers to the county official, '回邑' means 'returned to the town,' and '厚恤' means 'generously comforted.'
  • 命治棺往敛 - Means 'ordered the preparation of coffins and went to collect the bodies.' '命治棺' means 'ordered the preparation of coffins,' and '往敛' means 'went to collect the bodies.'
  • 仍即彼处立小庙 - Means 'still set up a small temple at that place.' '仍即' means 'still set up,' and '彼处' means 'that place.'
  • 犬凝立经日不食 - Means 'the dogs stood still for days without eating.' '凝立' means 'stood still,' and '经日' means 'for days.'
  • 见家人来摇尾迎道至尸所 - Means 'they saw the family members coming and wagged their tails to meet them at the place of the bodies.' '摇尾' means 'wagged their tails,' and '迎道至尸所' means 'met them at the place of the bodies.'
  • 宣既归塟 - Means 'Xuan then buried them.' '归塟' means 'buried.'
  • 犬亦死 - Means 'the dogs also died.' '亦' means 'also.'

  • 率家人登舟趋后山 - Literal expression meaning 'to lead the family onto a boat and head towards the back mountain,' used to describe the family's evacuation efforts.

  • 回望故庐已荡然随流而去 - Literal expression meaning 'looking back, the old house had already been swept away by the current,' used to describe the destruction of the house.

  • 迨水退往视则陷为汚泽 - Literal expression meaning 'when the water receded, it was seen that it had turned into a swamp,' used to describe the aftermath of the flood.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 源不穷 - Classical expression meaning 'the source is inexhaustible,' often used to describe something that is abundant or endless.
  • 浸浸闻于外 - Means 'gradually becoming known outside,' indicating that something is spreading or becoming public knowledge.
  • 掇买悉拒弗与 - A phrase meaning 'refused to sell or give anything,' emphasizing the refusal to part with something.
  • 畏人盗取 - Means 'fearing theft by others,' indicating concern about potential theft.
  • 设短墙阑护之 - A phrase meaning 'set up a short wall to protect it,' describing a protective measure.
  • 邻人愤嫉 - Means 'neighbors were envious and resentful,' indicating negative feelings towards the owner.
  • 夜逾墙入 - A phrase meaning 'at night, they climbed over the wall,' describing an act of trespassing.
  • 将空其根枿 - Means 'to strip the roots bare,' indicating the act of removing all parts of something.
  • 楮忽作人言 - A phrase meaning 'the fungus suddenly spoke like a person,' describing an unusual event where the fungus spoke.
  • 此非尔所得食 - Means 'this is not what you should eat,' indicating a prohibition.
  • 强取之必受殃灾 - A phrase meaning 'forcing it will bring disaster,' warning against taking something by force.
  • 我即昔时庵主也 - Means 'I am the former master of the temple,' identifying the speaker.
  • 坐虚受供施不知慙退 - A phrase meaning 'received offerings without merit and did not feel ashamed to withdraw,' describing a past wrongdoing.
  • 身没之后司罚为菌蕈以偿 - Means 'after death, I was punished by the underworld to become a fungus to repay,' indicating a supernatural punishment.
  • 所以肥美者吾精血所化也 - A phrase meaning 'the reason for its richness is that it is transformed from my essence and blood,' explaining the origin of the fungus's qualities.
  • 今谪数已足従此去矣 - Means 'now my punishment is complete, and I will leave from here,' indicating the end of a period of punishment.
  • 骇而退 - A phrase meaning 'frightened and retreated,' describing the reaction of the neighbors.
  • 以告汪汪 - Means 'and told Wang Wang,' indicating the act of informing someone.
  • 犹不信 - A phrase meaning 'still did not believe,' indicating disbelief.
  • 自往验之 - Means 'went to check it himself,' describing an action taken to verify something.
  • 不复有菌 - A phrase meaning 'there was no fungus left,' indicating the absence of the fungus.
  • 遂伐以为薪 - Means 'and cut it down to use as firewood,' describing the final action taken with the fungus.
  • 梁起道知处州 - Means 'Liang Qidao served as the governor of Quzhou,' indicating a historical figure and his position.
  • 由大理丞坐事送吏部注雩都知县 - A phrase meaning 'due to an official matter, he was transferred to the Ministry of Personnel and appointed as the magistrate of Yudu,' describing a career move.
  • 初到诣郡参谒既毕当趋下循廊而出 - Means 'upon arriving, he paid respects to the local officials, and after the ceremony, he was supposed to leave by the corridor,' describing a formal greeting.
  • 梁以其方为朝士且与之有旧 - A phrase meaning 'Liang considered him a fellow official and had known him for a long time,' indicating a personal relationship.
  • 留使升车 - Means 'he asked him to climb into the carriage,' describing an action taken by Liang.
  • 辞避不敢 - A phrase meaning 'refused to climb, saying he was afraid,' indicating a refusal.
  • 呼其字曰希韩 - Means 'called him by his courtesy name, Xi Han,' indicating a form of address.
  • 不须如此 - A phrase meaning 'don't be like that,' indicating a request for relaxation.
  • 言之再三 - Means 'said it several times,' indicating repeated requests.
  • 客将谓其官称 - A phrase meaning 'the attendants thought it was his official title,' indicating a misunderstanding.
  • 客主皆解颐 - A phrase meaning 'both the guest and the host laughed,' indicating amusement.
  • 赋性严毅 - Means 'with a strict and stern nature,' describing Liang's character.
  • 察其愚野 - A phrase meaning 'seeing that he was naive and unsophisticated,' indicating an assessment of the person.
  • 不之问 - Means 'did not question him further,' indicating a decision not to pursue the matter.
  • 李正之提防坑冶巡厯广西过长沙郡僚具迎牍称曰提防大正 - A phrase meaning 'Li Zheng, who was on a tour of inspection of mines and smelting in Guangxi, passed through Changsha, and the local officials prepared a document to greet him, calling him 'Tidian Dazheng,' describing a formal greeting.
  • 不知其名而亦误以为官称若寺正之者 - Means 'not knowing his name and mistakenly thinking it was his official title, similar to the title of a temple official,' indicating a misunderstanding.
  • 怒移文潭府治诸曹书吏 - A phrase meaning 'angry, he sent a letter to the governor of Tanfu to punish the officials and clerks,' describing an action taken in response to the misunderstanding.
  • 张敬夫居于潭其缄亦如是 - Means 'Zhang Jingfu, who lived in Tan, also wrote in the same manner,' indicating a similar action by another person.
  • 府主刘共甫笑之曰他人容或不晓君何为尔 - A phrase meaning 'the governor, Liu Gongfu, laughed and said, 'Others might not understand, but why did you do it?'' indicating a reaction to the misunderstanding.

  • 及李至引咎谢不敏焉 - A phrase meaning 'when Li arrived, he apologized and said he was not capable,' indicating a formal apology.

  • 顔名叔平鲁子侍郎之子防补登科年少隽爽遽至陨命士友嗟惜 - Yan's name was Shuping, the son of Lü Zi, the vice minister, who was promoted to the imperial examination at a young age, bright and lively, but suddenly died, causing grief among his friends and colleagues.
  • 岂其受命于天而为一神所夺抑非偶然耶 - Was it that he was destined by heaven to be taken by one god, or was it not a mere coincidence?
  • 江宁尉司一庙亦如此凡居官者至必礼谒丹阳刘平国宰独不肯加敬毎轿过其前但举袖掩面一弟随侍未防以小疾终妻陶氏懐姙而疾梦神言汝夫无礼于我我已取厥弟茍为不悛当复取汝陶氏以告刘刘不信陶果死刘始悔惧躬往祷谢乃已 - The Jiangning Magistrate's temple was also like this. All officials who came must pay respects. The magistrate of Danyang, Liu Pingguo, alone refused to show respect. Whenever his sedan chair passed by the temple, he would raise his sleeve to cover his face. His younger brother, who was accompanying him, died of a minor illness. His wife, Tao, was pregnant and fell ill, dreaming that a god said, 'Your husband was disrespectful to me, and I have already taken your younger brother. If he does not repent, I will take you too.' Tao told Liu about the dream, but he did not believe her. Tao eventually died, and Liu began to regret and fear, going himself to pray and apologize, and then the matter was resolved.
  • 范成绩石湖参政弟也赋性坚暴每従其兄居藩方辄为所困挠绍熙甲寅为建康通判冬至之夕庖妾报甑鸣一家皆惧欲求僧巫禳谢置不问既而益甚至或哮吼作鸡犬百禽之声其音响厉外间悉闻之范亲以刀破其甑即日声出釡中又碎釡毁灶于是诸怪互作正对客坐桌椅昻然自举烘笼奋而行蹴之不仆舞跃自若凡扰扰数月习惯为常次年四月八日与同僚在都防忽觉痰不复能支吾従吏掖登车仅到家喉中涎如泉涌吚嚘有声其家人云全与向来相似众医切脉下药皆不可纳俄大叫一声而殂 - Fan Chengji was the younger brother of the Shihu Censor. He had a stubborn and violent nature. Whenever he followed his elder brother to reside in a remote place, he was always troubled and obstructed. In the year Shaoshi Jia Yin, he was appointed as the Deputy Magistrate of Jiankang. On the evening of the Winter Solstice, the cook's maid reported that the steamer was making strange noises. The whole family was afraid and wanted to seek help from monks and sorcerers to ward off the evil, but Fan ignored it. The strange noises became more intense, sometimes roaring like chickens, dogs, and other animals. The sounds were loud and could be heard outside. Fan personally broke the steamer with a knife. That day, the sounds came from the pot, and the pot and stove were broken. Various strange phenomena occurred, and the table and chairs stood upright by themselves. The heating stove moved and jumped, but it did not fall. It danced and leaped as if nothing was wrong. The disturbances lasted for several months, and the family gradually got used to it. The next year on the 8th day of the 4th month, while he was with his colleagues in the capital, he suddenly felt a severe cough and could no longer bear it. His attendants helped him get on the carriage, and he barely reached home. There was a stream of saliva in his throat, and he made a sound like 'yiyi.' His family said it was exactly like before. Many doctors took his pulse and prescribed medicine, but none could be taken. Soon, he let out a loud cry and died.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long, unbroken sentence describes a series of events: the woman contracting an illness, summoning a physician, the woman suddenly behaving like a man and claiming to be a deity, scolding the physician by name, and the physician's reaction. The sentence is elliptical and requires careful parsing to maintain clarity in English.
  • This phrase can be read as 'your family has always been strict in their worship,' but it could also imply a deeper spiritual connection or favor from the gods.
  • This phrase can be interpreted as 'I feel your sincerity,' but it could also imply a divine recognition of the family's piety.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 少孤寡无依 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'young, widowed, and without support,' emphasizing her vulnerable state.
  • 陈批谕使投诚观音大士 - The sentence can be read as 'Chen advised her to surrender to the Bodhisattva Guanyin,' suggesting a religious practice.
  • 冀或慈怜 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'hoping for compassion,' expressing the hope for divine intervention.
  • 晨夕礼拜不怠 - The phrase can be read as 'worshiping morning and evening without tiring,' indicating her persistent religious devotion.
  • 假寐如入定状 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'falling into a meditative state while napping,' describing a deep, trance-like sleep.
  • 必见端严瑞相 - The phrase can be read as 'always seeing a dignified and auspicious appearance,' referring to visions of Guanyin.
  • 训诲拳拳 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'admonishing earnestly,' indicating the intensity of the guidance received.
  • 劝以作礼西方阿弥陀佛 - The phrase can be read as 'encouraged her to pay respects to Amitabha Buddha in the West,' suggesting a shift in her religious focus.
  • 亲授四句偈 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'personally taught her four lines of verse,' referring to a set of Buddhist teachings.
  • 净土周沙界 - The phrase can be read as 'the Pure Land encompasses all realms,' a concept in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 云何独礼西 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'why should one only pay respects to the West,' questioning the exclusivity of Western reverence.
  • 但能囘一念 - The phrase can be read as 'if one can turn a single thought,' emphasizing the power of mental transformation.
  • 触处是菩提 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'everywhere is enlightenment,' a key Buddhist teaching.
  • 可普劝持诵 - The phrase can be read as 'can be widely encouraged to recite,' indicating the spread of the teachings.
  • 曽未逾月二患顿愈 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'within a month, both illnesses suddenly healed,' highlighting the rapid recovery.
  • 不假医药之力 - The phrase can be read as 'without the help of medicine,' emphasizing the supernatural nature of the healing.
  • 陈谓其力愈纯 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'Chen said her faith became purer,' indicating the strengthening of her spiritual practice.
  • 应答如响 - The phrase can be read as 'responded as if by echo,' describing her improved ability to communicate.
  • 为镂板以广其传 - The phrase can be interpreted as 'had it carved on a woodblock to spread it widely,' indicating the dissemination of her experience.

  • 旦而行可二十里 - '旦而行' means 'to start traveling in the morning,' and '可二十里' means 'about twenty li.' This phrase describes the distance traveled in the morning.

  • 银在手中初未尝启视也 - '银在手中' means 'the silver was in her hand,' and '初未尝启视也' means 'she had never opened it to look at it.' This phrase describes the woman's behavior with the silver.

People rushed to grab them, but they were swallowed by the pond and could not be retrieved. This is a supernatural event where objects vanish into the pond.
  • 绍兴三年近居村民姚叟当秋夜闻钟声铿訇响振山谷偕二子乗月往观之正见一钟坠于潭潭之侧三寺环绕僧知其异明旦各持诵经咒投牒请祷冀得钟左右二寺皆邈然弗应惟中寺才掷牒水即涌髙丈余钟随而浮上僧具威仪梵呗迎以归其重三千斤双龙蟠结精巧特甚安奉于寺楼上继此一潭帖然不复有异后数岁寺遭火一空独钟楼屹存 - In the third year of Shaoxing, a villager named Yao Lao, who lived nearby, heard the sound of a bell at night in autumn. The sound was loud and resonated through the mountains and valleys. He went with his two sons to see it, and they saw a bell fall into the pond. On both sides of the pond, there were three temples, and the monks knew it was unusual. The next morning, they each held scriptures, chanted spells, and submitted petitions, hoping to retrieve the bell. The temples on both sides did not respond, but the middle temple, as soon as they threw the petition, the water rose more than ten feet, and the bell floated up. The monks prepared with solemn ceremonies and chants, and the bell was brought back. It weighed three thousand pounds, with two dragons intertwined, exquisitely crafted. It was enshrined on the temple’s upper floor. After this, the pond remained calm and no longer showed any abnormalities. Several years later, the temple was burned down, leaving only the bell tower standing. This is a detailed account of a supernatural event involving a bell and a temple.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes a snake handler who kept a fish-like creature in a clay pot. The creature has black color, two hands like a strong man, and ten fingers, which is described as a 'mermaid.'
  • This sentence describes a large snake found in the mountains of Wuchang, which is called 'Jianjiu' (Sword Spine Bird). When it encounters a person, it raises its head and stands still, and its tail touches the ground, barely an inch above the ground. One must quickly avoid it to avoid harm.
  • This section describes the history of the Yousheng Palace on Bian Mountain, including the construction by Shen Chongzhen, the discovery of a miraculous stone, and the temple's elevation to a higher status by the emperor.

  • 洪应贤 - The name of the protagonist, Hong Yingxian, a clerk from Wuyuan County.
  • 江佐 - The name of the character who died, Jiang Zuo, a clerk.
  • 仲堪 - The name of Hong Yingxian's son, Zhongkan.
  • 江侁 - The name of Jiang Zuo's younger brother, Jiang Shēn.
  • 王伋 - The name of the former county official, Wang Ji.
